purpose: Define authority, write-lease, artifact, handoff, and role-transition rules for ADEU-governed Codex multi-role execution.
canonical_precedence: This JSON artifact is the canonical contract surface. The lock and policy docs are derived interpretive layers and must not weaken or contradict this contract.

architectural_positioning.governance_axis: This contract family governs role authority and execution jurisdiction. It is orthogonal to the V33/V34 pipeline-contract family, which governs deterministic harness stages such as compile, verify, package, sign, and parity.
architectural_positioning.control_execution_split: ADEU is the control plane and Codex is the execution plane.

platform_assumption: Current Codex provides real session, tool, and sub-agent primitives but does not hard-enforce these role boundaries; ADEU orchestration is authoritative.

authoritative.definition: Artifacts that can change implementation truth or ADEU governance truth.

advisory.definition: Artifacts that inform judgment but do not by themselves change implementation or governance truth.

scratch.definition: Ephemeral or local working artifacts with no authority unless explicitly promoted.

implementation.definition: Authoritative artifacts that change implementation truth inside the implementation repo.

governance.definition: Authoritative artifacts that change ADEU governance truth.

promotion_rule: No advisory or scratch artifact becomes authoritative without explicit main_orchestrator acceptance.

cross_role_artifact_execution_policy.rule: Executing an advisory or scratch artifact consumes the authority of the executor, not the author.
cross_role_artifact_execution_policy.builder_liability_rule: If a builder executes a support-role script or other artifact, all resulting mutations are attributed to the builder's implementation write lease.

allowed_actions: interpret ADEU lock and governance state
decide PR readiness, merge readiness, and closeout readiness
forbidden_actions: treat advisory worker output as self-authenticating

allowed_actions: edit production code within leased scope
prepare branch, commit, and PR updates

forbidden_actions: edit authoritative implementation artifacts by default

allowed_actions: run tests, lint, build, repro, and targeted checks
forbidden_actions: edit authoritative implementation artifacts by default
modify ADEU governance artifacts by default

write_lease_rules: There is exactly one exclusive authoritative implementation write lease by default.
The builder holds the current write lease for the assigned scope only.
The write lease is bounded by scope, time, and ownership surface.
The main_orchestrator may revoke, narrow, or transfer the lease explicitly.
Support roles may not self-upgrade into lease holders.
If a support role must edit authoritative implementation artifacts, it must be explicitly re-roled and granted a new lease.
Prior advisory output from a re-roled worker remains advisory unless separately accepted.

transition_rules: Any transition that changes authoritative write rights requires explicit main_orchestrator approval.
Every transition must declare new role, new scope_owned, and whether authoritative write access is granted.
A support role becoming a builder is a new lease event, not an implicit continuation.
A builder losing scope or authority must have the lease explicitly revoked or narrowed.
Role transition records are part of orchestration state and may be cited in closeout reasoning.

shared_handoff_envelope.trust_model: Worker-supplied handoff envelopes are self-reported claims until main_orchestrator reconciles them against observed repo state, command output, and emitted artifacts.

field_policies.authority_level: must reflect current contract role, not claimed importance
field_policies.authority_domain: must be one of implementation, governance, or advisory
field_policies.artifact_class: must classify produced output as authoritative, advisory, or scratch
field_policies.artifact_surface: must identify the relevant surface as implementation, governance, mixed, or none
field_policies.evidence_refs: must point to concrete files, logs, or command outputs where applicable
field_policies.blocking_state: must be either blocking or non_blocking and must describe urgency to the orchestrator, not subjective effort
field_policies.escalation_reason: always required; must be an empty string when no escalation exists and a non-empty explanation when escalation exists
field_policies.files_changed: self-reported and non-authoritative until reconciled against observed repo state
field_policies.commands_run: self-reported and non-authoritative until reconciled against observed execution evidence
field_policies.artifacts_produced: self-reported and non-authoritative until reconciled against actual emitted artifacts

minimum_checks: recompute effective files_changed from observed repo diff or equivalent state witness
compare claimed scope_owned against observed mutation surface
verify evidence_refs resolve to actual files, logs, or outputs where applicable
reject lease-scope sufficiency claims based on self-report alone

acceptance_boundary: Only main_orchestrator may decide that evidence is sufficient for acceptance.
Validator may report pass, fail, or unresolved gaps, but may not declare acceptance.
Explorer may report likely truth, but may not declare acceptance.
Builder may report implementation completion within scope, but may not declare slice acceptance or closure.
Docs_helper may draft explanatory text, but may not generate authoritative closeout language.
default_rules: Single builder is the default for one implementation repo.
Multiple writers are forbidden unless explicitly authorized by main_orchestrator.
Support roles should prefer advisory and evidence output over direct mutation.
ADEU remains the governance and closeout authority layer.
